title: Call for Climate Doers and Thinkers
text:
I’m excited to be teaching the Energy and Climate Policy graduate class in the Fall semester and the Energy, Climate, and Society undergraduate class in the Spring semester, both on Monday evenings. I’m seeking guest speakers who are passionate about sharing their experiences and inspiring next generation of climate leaders.
We’re exploring two key themes:

Climate and Career: Share your journey working in climate-focused roles, discussing what led you to this path and how your work contributes to meaningful climate solutions.
Climate and Community: Highlight impactful activities that bring climate actions to the community level, showcasing the power of local engagement.

We always welcome exciting new research, so if you have fresh insights or innovative projects, we’d love to hear from you as well.

What you’ll provide: A 30-minute virtual sharing session plus Q&A.
What you’ll get: A highlight on our course webpage and the opportunity to influence and possibly change the career paths of our students.

If interested, please reach out to me by email or messages with your ideas, and the students will decide which topics aligns with their interests. We are particularly interested in hearing earlier career professionals and those doing hands-on work in the field.
Let’s collaborate to empower and inspire the climate leaders of tomorrow.

title: Richard Morse: Renewable Energy Investment
text:

title: Richard Morse: Renewable Energy Investment
text:
About
Global clean energy supply chains stand at a critical intersection of integration and decoupling. The panel will discuss the evolving landscape, benefits and challenges, and future directions of clean energy global supply chains, to meet climate goals while addressing growing international and domestic concerns.
